title: Analyses
description: The Analyses section is the primary workspace for designing, executing, and reviewing OHDSI-standard analyses across your observational data. It supports characterization studies, incidence rate calculations, treatment pathway analyses, population-level estimation, patient-level prediction, and the full study lifecycle from design through evidence synthesis and package export.
tips: Start with a Characterization analysis to understand baseline demographics and clinical features of your cohort before moving to causal or predictive analyses.
Use the Study Designer to document study objectives, exposure definitions, and outcome definitions in one place before generating analytical packages.
Population-Level Estimation and Patient-Level Prediction analyses require pre-built cohort definitions — ensure your cohorts are validated before running these modules.
Treatment Pathways analyses work best when drug era collapse logic and observation window settings are reviewed against your clinical question.
Export a Study Package to share a fully reproducible analysis bundle with collaborators who have access to their own OMOP CDM databases.
